libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` for JVM test execution and native-image build arguments when tests exercise Guice method interception/AOP/proxy generation. Regenerate tests against `2.9.1` rather than reusing `2.1.7` internals: classes such as `MapMaker`, `MethodProxy`, and `FinalizableReferenceQueue` are present under `$`-prefixed internal binary names in `2.9.1`, so tests should prefer public Guice APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Basic injector/binding coverage works with the normal resolved transitive dependencies, but AOP/interceptor tests fail at runtime without the `java.lang` module opening.
Tests importing old unprefixed internal Guice classes from `2.1.7` will continue to fail javac against `2.9.1`.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but meaningful coverage of sisu-guice AOP/proxy generation on modern JDKs requires opening java.lang for reflective CGLIB class definition.
